description:
To properly set the owner of /var/log/(b|w)tmp(.*|-*), run the command: 
$ sudo chown root /var/log/(b|w)tmp(.*|-*)
rationale:
The <tt>/var/log/(b|w)tmp(.*|-*)</tt> file contains logs of reports the most recent login of all users
and should only be accessed by authorized personnel.
ocil_clause: /var/log/(b|w)tmp(.*|-*) does not have an owner of root
ocil:
To check the ownership of /var/log/(b|w)tmp(.*|-*), run the command: 
$ ls -lL /var/log/(b|w)tmp(.*|-*)
If properly configured, the output should indicate the following owner: root
fixtext:
To properly set the owner of /var/log/(b|w)tmp(.*|-*), run the command: 
$ sudo chown root /var/log/(b|w)tmp(.*|-*)
srg_requirement: The Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 /var/log/(b|w)tmp(.*|-*) file must be owned by root.
title: Verify User Who Owns /var/log/(b|w)tmp(.*|-*) File
